books.title: Drawing, Design, and Craft-Work
books.author: Frederick J. Glass
books.rationale: This is an excellent guide to art instruction, with suggestions for how to begin, materials to use, and the directions future work may take. This book is profusely illustrated with clear explanations.
books.noteText: The free link is a different edition, and the lesson plans will have different page numbers. Download the file to view the free version. It will likely appear blank in the PDF reader.

books.author: Marion Hudson
books.rationale: This book is perfect for those who are new to brush drawing. The exercises on gridded paper help students learn to mix colors and control the brush. Brushforms ("blobs") are used to create designs.

books.title: Redeeming Vision
books.author: Elissa Weichbrodt
books.rationale: Educators of all levels and high school students take the next step in deepening their relationship with art by contemplating a theology of art for viewers which impacts how we see as well as how we are and how we love.  Practice in taking what has been learned in previous years along with new aspects of art elements and themes is guided by Dr. Weichbrodt.

books.title: Art Print Set
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This art print set contains high-resolution images that are in the public domain or used for Alveary with permission. The Art Print Set contains paintings by each term's artist: Edward Mitchell Bannister, Vincent van Gogh, and Lilias Trotter. We recommend printing on cardstock. Hard copies will be available with lesson plans.
books.noteText: Selections are also included in Our Work packets for Grades 1-12.

books.title: Our Work: Grades 1-2
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This is a compilation of the year's Recitation, Picture Study, Hymns, Spirituals, and Folk Songs. Line drawings of artifacts from the historical time period are also included to provide students with inspiration for their Book of Centuries. We recommend printing or purchasing a copy for each student.

books.title: A Passion for the Impossible: The Life of Lilias Trotter
books.author: Miriam Rockness
books.rationale: An inspiring biography of Lilias Trotter, a British artist who forsook fame to devote her life to Christian missionary work in Algeria, guided by faith, creativity, and love.

books.title: Van Gogh
books.author: Ingo Walther & Rainer Metzger
books.rationale: This comprehensive study of Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890) pairs a detailed monograph on his life and art with a complete catalogue of his 871 paintings.
books.noteText: Please note there is some minor nudity, and Van Gogh lived a tumultuous life, ultimately committing suicide.

books.title: Old Testament Today
books.author: John H. Walton & Andrew E. Hill
books.rationale: It is so important for students to understand the context in which the Bible was written and what it meant to the original audience. However, it is also important to understand how the OT writings are relevant to us today. The authors do a wonderful job communicating all these ideas. Lesson plans will guide you in navigating this rich text.
books.noteText: Students will use this book for several years as they read through the OT.

books.title: NIV Cultural Backgrounds Study Bible: Bringing to Life the Ancient World of Scripture
books.author: Zondervan
books.rationale: Contains unabridged Scripture along with important archaeological, historical, and cultural background.
books.noteText: This resource is for Grades 9+ ONLY. Grades 7-8 should use the ESV Concise Study Bible.
books.maySubText: a translation of your choice.
books.sharedText:
Old Testament: Grades 7-12
New Testament: Grade 9
New Testament: Grades 10-11
New Testament: Grade 12

books.title: ESV Concise Study Bible
books.rationale: A smaller-sized study Bible that is appropriate for this age. We highly suggest this Bible for the insightful study notes and accessibility for this age group. During lesson time, students will be asked to review the study notes and other explanatory material.
books.noteText: This resource is for Grades 7-8 ONLY. Grades 9+ should use Cultural Backgrounds Study Bible.
books.maySubText: a translation of your choice but it is important to have one with study notes.
books.sharedText:
Old Testament: Grades 7-12
New Testament: Grades 7-8

books.rationale: This is a simple, inexpensive Bible atlas that provides full-page maps of the biblical lands of the Old and New Testaments. In Bible lessons, students will be directed to look at specific pages. This resource is for Grades 3-8 ONLY.
books.maySubText: other Bible atlas, though page numbers in lesson plans will not match.

books.title: Introducing the New Testament: A Historical, Literary, and Theological Survey
books.author: Mark Allan Powell
books.rationale: An overview of the NT for older students and educators providing book by book background and major themes to be used for years. The chapters can be read in any order allowing for use anywhere in the reading rotation. Powell presents various perspectives on interpretive issues to encourage engagement with ideas but does not attempt to resolve all the issues.  Artwork through the centuries adds a unique element that illustrates themes and demonstrates the influence of the NT over the centuries.
books.noteText: Students will use this book for several years as they read through the NT.
books.sharedText:
New Testament: Grades 7-8
New Testament: Grade 9
New Testament: Grades 10-11
New Testament: Grade 12

books.title: Renewal: The Church That Expands Outward
books.author: Luke H. Davis
books.rationale: Students will read the last half of the book this year as they continue to see how the church expanded globally through preaching, mission work, and social work in the name of Christ. The author tells the story of a pivotal event in the life of faithful Christians including William Wilberforce, Sojourner Truth, Hudson Taylor and more. Lesson plans will also include necessary background information, links, and other pertinent PDFs and articles.
books.noteText: Use option one link to receive discount.

books.title: Spurgeon: A Biography
books.author: Arnold Dallimore
books.rationale: This biography provides an introduction to Charles Spurgeon (1834-1892), an influential preacher who started the Metropolitan Tabernacle in London, England. Spurgeon is often called “The Prince of Preachers,” and this biography shows his life of faith, his personal struggles with depression, his humor, and his love for his family as he strived to preach the gospel so that all might hear.

books.title: An Honorable Heritage: The Pandita Ramabai Story in Her Own Words
books.author: Pandita Ramabai
books.rationale: Pandita Ramabai (1858-1922) was a social reformer, an advocate for the emancipation of women, and a pioneer in education. She tells her testimony of being born into a privileged Brahman caste in India and leaving it all behind to become a Christian. It is a fascinating read!
books.noteText: Use option one link to receive discount.

books.title: Transformed by Truth
books.author: Katherine Forster
books.rationale: This book is written for students to encourage them to make Bible reading a special time of their day. The author demonstrates the practices of observation, interpretation, and application so students can discover how God's truth can literally transform them from the inside out. These practices will also be implemented during the NT Bible lessons.

books.title: This Changes Everything
books.author: Jaquelle Ferris
books.rationale: This book speaks directly to teenagers, showing how the gospel shapes identity, purpose, and everyday choices. The author challenges the idea that faith is something to “grow into later” and instead invites young readers to take their beliefs seriously now.

books.title: Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.
books.author: Rand McNally
books.rationale: Presents important periods and turning points in 5,000 years of history. Thematic maps include literacy, languages, religions, and more. Used across curriculum.

books.title: The Plutarch Project Volume Three (Revised)
books.author: Anne White
books.rationale: Students will read about the lives of Julius Caesar, Agis and Cleomenes, and the Gracchi. Anne White has broken up the Lives into reasonable sections with suitable omissions for length and content. She also included study helps to aid with understanding. Lesson plans will offer guidance as well.
books.sharedText:
Plutarch: Grades 6-8
Plutarch/Government: Grade 9 (U.S.)
Plutarch/Government: Grade 9 (Canada)

books.title: Reading PDF
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Students will learn about important historical documents developed between 1800-1900. Following the founding of the country, the framework of the nation begins to take shape. The Missouri Compromise sets the stage for the Civil War as new states join the nation; the federal government experiences internal conflict as the Executive Branch disregards the Judicial Branch over the sovereignty of tribal nations; and the Reconstruction Amendments are added to the Constitution.  A "Reading PDF" will be included in the lesson plans that provides accessible analysis integrated with relevant excerpts of Supreme Court decisions, legislative acts, and other texts as appropriate.
books.sharedText:
American Gov./ Economics: Grade 8
Plutarch/Government: Grade 9 (U.S.)
Econ/Civics: Grade 10 (U.S.)
Econ/Civics: Grade 11 (U.S.)

books.title: Dollars and Sense: A Kid's Guide to Using - Not Losing - Money
books.author: Elaine Scott
books.rationale: In this introduction to Economics, students will learn about the history of money, the banking system, and personal finance.
books.sharedText:
American Gov./ Economics: Grade 8
Canadian Gov./ Economics: Grade 8

books.title: Ourselves: Book 1
books.author: Charlotte Mason
books.rationale: Mason emphasized that children are persons; this is the volume in which she defines that term. What characteristics do we, as Image-Bearers, have in common? What kinds of pitfalls should we anticipate? These are some of the profound questions Mason deals with in this, her only volume written directly to students.
books.maySubText: any edition.

books.title: Canada's Constitutional Monarchy
books.author: Nathan Tidridge
books.rationale: Brimming with photographs and archival images, this book, written by a high school history teacher, is an accessible and extensive account of the role played by the Crown in Canada's history, Constitution, system of parliamentary government, other institutions, and the day-to-day lives of all Canadians.
books.noteText: This book is used over two years (Grades 8 and 9).

books.title: Our States Have Crazy Shapes
books.author: Lynn Garthwaite
books.rationale: Short chapter vignettes will explain how states ultimately received their shapes.  Each state has a story and reason for having the border lines they do.  Students will sketch their own maps based on what they learn.

books.title: United States Atlas 7th Ed.
books.author: National Geographic Kids
books.rationale: This atlas is used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.

books.title: Westward with Fremont: The Story of Solomon Carvalho
books.author: Sophie Greenspan
books.rationale: This book tells the true story of Solomon Nunes Carvalho, a Jewish artist and early photographer who joined explorer John C. Frémont on a dangerous expedition across the American West in the 1850s.  Students will come to see how maps were made from firsthand observation and why accurate geographic knowledge mattered for exploration and expansion. It is a fascinating read that follows the challenges of exploration—extreme weather, lack of supplies, illness, and difficult terrain.
books.noteText: Use option one link to receive discount.

books.title: This Strange Wilderness: The Life and Art of John James Audubon
books.author: Nancy Plain
books.rationale: It tells the story of Audubon’s (1785-1851) life as a naturalist, artist, and explorer in early America. The book follows his journeys through forests, swamps, and frontier settlements as he studied and painted North American birds in their natural habitats.
books.sharedText:
Geography: Grade 7 (U.S.)
Geography: Grade 8 (U.S.)

books.title: Canadian Student Atlas
books.author: Collins Maps
books.rationale: Used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.
books.noteText: For Canadian students only. Optional for Grade 1.
books.maySubText: other atlas; however, map work and other lessons refer specifically to this one.
books.sharedText:
Geography: Grade 8 (Canada)
Geography: Grade 7 (Canada)

books.title: Draw the Natural Wonders of Canada and Greenland: Geography Disguised as Fun
books.author: Kristin Draeger
books.rationale: After having mapped each of the Canadian provinces and territories - learning their boundaries, capitals, and major landmarks - students will then use this book for a more in-depth exploration of regional geography. Working from map templates, students will proceed to learn about and map the mountains, rivers, and lakes in Canada, and illustrate their maps with drawings of local flora and fauna.

books.title: Vanished Beyond the Map
books.author: Adam Shoalts
books.rationale: Vanished Beyond the Map unfolds like a mystery novel: after first stumbling upon the name Hubert Darrell in an old encyclopedia, Adam Shoalts sets off on a journey through the arctic and the archives to discover as much as he can about this nearly-forgotten explorer, mapmaker and guide who disappeared in 1910. This book is remarkable for showing readers what is involved in trying to solve a historical mystery: where do you find clues? what kinds of questions do you ask? how much can we really know about the past? But it is also a book which situates Darrell within the wider world of geographers and explorers in the late-nineteenth century, who were attempting to complete the map of the world by charting the unknown lands of the arctic and antarctic.
books.noteText: This book is assigned for both Geography: Grade 8 (Canada) and History: Grade 9-10 (Canada). Note the possible overlap if you are combining students in either of these courses.
books.sharedText:
Geography: Grade 8 (Canada)
Canadian History: Grades 9-10

books.title: Sacajawea
books.author: Joseph Bruchac
books.rationale: By blending careful historical research with storytelling, Bruchac presents the Lewis and Clark expedition through an Indigenous perspective. This novel helps students see Sacajawea as a complex, intelligent young woman whose language skills, cultural knowledge, and resilience were essential to the expedition’s survival.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: A History of US: Liberty for All?: 1820-1860
books.author: Joy Hakim
books.rationale: This volume is a great spine that covers the times and events between 1820-1860. Through the use of engaging narrative, photographs and primary sources, Hakim helps students get a feel for the US during a time of westward expansion, railroad building, and industrialization.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Abraham Lincoln and Frederick Douglass: The Story Behind and American Friendship
books.author: Russell Freedman
books.rationale: This book explores the Civil War era through the evolving relationship of these two highly influential men. It is a fascinating look into how real people grappled with the moral issues of the day.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Gold Mountain
books.author: Betty G. Yee
books.rationale: This historical fiction book introduces students to the Chinese immigrant experience in the 1800’s when thousands of Chinese workers came to the US to work on the Transcontinental Railroad. It is the story of a young Chinese girl who disguises herself as a boy and takes her deceased brother’s contract to work for the Central Pacific railroad. This book helps students get a better understanding of Chinese culture, the hardships and discrimination Chinese people faced, and the hard work it took to create the transcontinental railroad.

books.title: A Year in the South: 1865
books.author: Stephen Ash
books.rationale: This true, nonfiction account uses letters and diaries to tell the stories of four different people over the course of 1865–both during and right after the Civil War ends. It gives the student a look into the lives of people who were maneuvering through a tumultuous change. It encourages students to see that Reconstruction was a complex time that didn’t mean instant equality or peace.
books.noteText: The students will not read all four stories.

books.title: In the Shadow of the Alamo
books.author: Sherry Garland
books.rationale: By reading In the Shadow of the Alamo, students will be able to step into the shoes of a fifteen-year-old boy named Lorenzo Bonifacio who is plucked up by his government and has to fight in the Texas Revolution and the Alamo. Along the way, he experiences great friendships and great loss. Through reading this fictional book, students will get to learn about Mexico and its complicated history with the United States.

books.title: Eiffel's Tower for Young People
books.author: Rebecca Steffof
books.rationale: Set in the late 1800s, the book follows engineer Gustave Eiffel and his team as they design and construct the tower for the 1889 Paris World’s Fair, a celebration of modern industry and innovation. The book places the Eiffel Tower in a global context, helping students see how world’s fairs, new technologies, and urban growth reflected broader historical shifts that were affecting many countries at the time. Students will also be introduced to other famous figures of that time including Annie Oakley, Dinah Salifou (king of Nalu people of Guinea), the Shah of Persia and the Van Gogh brothers. Architecture will also be integrated into the lesson plans.

books.title: Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.
books.author: Rand McNally
books.rationale: Presents important periods and turning points in 5,000 years of history. Thematic maps include literacy, languages, religions, and more. Used across curriculum.

Plutarch: Grades 6-8
First Citizen Stories
Ancient History: Grades 7-9

books.title: A Short History of Ancient Rome
books.author: Pascal Hughes
books.rationale: This book charts the dramatic twists and turns of the Roman kingdom, the republic, the empire and its decline. Pascal Hughes writes the history in a popular form that is engaging and easy to understand. Students will walk away from the reading with a deep understanding of one of the most powerful nations in the world.
books.noteText: The first few chapters contain sensitive content and will not be assigned to students. Next year's ancient history rotation is 100 B.C.-350 A.D. Therefore, students will begin with ch.6 when Sulla becomes dictator.

books.title: The Witness of Early Christian Women: Mothers of the Church
books.author: Mike Aquilina
books.rationale: Source documents/writings alongside short biographies of women and their impact in the early church.

books.title: Jeremy's War 1812
books.author: John Ibbitson
books.rationale: A gripping account of the War of 1812 told from the perspective of Jeremy Fields, a fifteen-year-old orphaned boy who joins a regiment of British soldiers and eventually becomes the personal servant of Sir Isaac Brock. Historical details blend smoothly with a fictional story to create a detailed recreation of this definitive period in Canada's pre-Confederation history.

books.title: Sophie's Rebellion
books.author: Beverley Boissery
books.rationale: Sophie's Rebellion is a fictional account of one American girl's experience of the 1837-1838 Rebellions, as she is held prisoner alongside her British travelling companions and befriends a young rebel named Luc. Through Sophie's eyes, the reader is able to see multiple perspectives of this conflict and is introduced to a number of notable historical figures. A trained historian, Beverley Boissery creates an authentically detailed account of Upper and Lower Canada in the mid-nineteenth century.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 9-10
Canadian History: Grades 7-8

books.title: The Story of Canada
books.author: Janet Lunn, Christopher Moore
books.rationale: Canadian history is told through rich narrative, recreations of daily life, folk tales, and fascinating facts.
books.maySubText: previous edition. Page numbers will differ from lesson plans.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 5-6
Canadian History: Grades 7-8

books.title: The Charlottetown Conference and the Birth of Confederation
books.author: Deirdre Kessler
books.rationale: The Charlottetown Conference and the Birth of Confederation is a detailed, engagingly-written account of the historical factors, issues, political players, and conferences which eventually led to Canada's Confederation in 1867. Attention is paid to the motives and pressures urging British North America towards unity, as well as the specific interests and context of each of the colonies before Confederation. The book brings this historical period to life with numerous photographs, paintings, archival documents, and other primary sources.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 7-8
Canadian History: Grades 9-10

books.title: Elijah of Buxton
books.author: Christopher Paul Curtis
books.rationale: This multi-award-winning novel brings to life the real town of Buxton, Ontario, a settlement of runaway slaves and free Black people that was a haven of safety and hope, especially in the years leading up to the Civil War. The story is told through the distinctive voice of Elijah, the first free-born child of Buxton, who is equal parts sensitive, naive, earnest, playful, and mischievous. When he is forced to discover the horrors of slavery first-hand, he demonstrates courage and maturity beyond his young age. Christopher Paul Curtis handles this difficult topic deftly, blending gentle humour and wisdom with a gripping plot of risk and danger.

books.title: In the Shadow of the Alamo
books.author: Sherry Garland
books.rationale: By reading In the Shadow of the Alamo, students will be able to step into the shoes of a fifteen-year-old boy named Lorenzo Bonifacio who is plucked up by his government and has to fight in the Texas Revolution and the Alamo. Along the way, he experiences great friendships and great loss. Through reading this fictional book, students will get to learn about Mexico and its complicated history with the United States.

books.title: Eiffel's Tower for Young People
books.author: Rebecca Steffof
books.rationale: Set in the late 1800s, the book follows engineer Gustave Eiffel and his team as they design and construct the tower for the 1889 Paris World’s Fair, a celebration of modern industry and innovation. The book places the Eiffel Tower in a global context, helping students see how world’s fairs, new technologies, and urban growth reflected broader historical shifts that were affecting many countries at the time. Students will also be introduced to other famous figures of that time including Annie Oakley, Dinah Salifou (king of Nalu people of Guinea), the Shah of Persia and the Van Gogh brothers. Architecture will also be integrated into the lesson plans.

books.title: Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.
books.author: Rand McNally
books.rationale: Presents important periods and turning points in 5,000 years of history. Thematic maps include literacy, languages, religions, and more. Used across curriculum.

books.title: A Short History of Ancient Rome
books.author: Pascal Hughes
books.rationale: This book charts the dramatic twists and turns of the Roman kingdom, the republic, the empire and its decline. Pascal Hughes writes the history in a popular form that is engaging and easy to understand. Students will walk away from the reading with a deep understanding of one of the most powerful nations in the world.
books.noteText: The first few chapters contain sensitive content and will not be assigned to students. Next year's ancient history rotation is 100 B.C.-350 A.D. Therefore, students will begin with ch.6 when Sulla becomes dictator.

books.title: The Witness of Early Christian Women: Mothers of the Church

books.title: The Lightning Dreamer: Cuba's Greatest Abolitionist
books.author: Margarita Engle
books.rationale: This book is a poetic fictionalized account of famous Cuban abolitionist and author Gertrudis Gómez Avellaneda (1814-1873). Since Avellaneda wrote in Spanish, this biography allows students to get a glimpse of a significant 19th century author outside the English canon. Engle's style also provides opportunities for students to consider the differences between poetry and prose and experiment with point of view in connected composition assignments.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: A Christmas Carol

books.rationale: As a famous work of 19th century literature, A Christmas Carol expands students' understanding of this historical period while also nourishing them with a thought-provoking tale about greed and generosity. It is scheduled in the mythology stream as Dicken's work has become a modern myth, shaping our conceptions of Christmas and more.

books.title: Little Women
books.author: Louisa May Alcott
books.rationale: A beloved piece of 19th century literature, this story follows four sisters as they grow and learn to live full and happy lives. The story is loosely set during the American Civil War and has remained popular since it was first published.

books.title: Sir Gibbie
books.author: George MacDonald
books.rationale: Although less well known than some of his friends, George Macdonald cast an outsized influence on his contemporaries and many 20th century writers who followed him, including C.S. Lewis and J.R.R. Tolkein. Macdonald's genius for portraying goodness and inspiring readers is on full display in this rags-to-riches tale of a mute boy from the Scotch Highlands.
books.noteText: Choose this OR Little Women.

books.title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
books.author: Frederick Douglass
books.rationale: Students read extended selections of this classic 19th century narrative which details the author's story from slavery to freedom. For US students, this text will enhance their reading of Lincoln & Douglass in US History.
books.noteText: GRADES 9+ should choose this OR Up From Slavery and Souls of Black Folk.

books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: This play is often called a Tragicomedy because it has both comedic and tragic elements. It is about an overthrown Duke and his daughter who are exiled on an island. The play addresses the themes of power, betrayal, magic, slavery, and the restoration of relationships.

books.title: The Winter's Tale
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: The Winter's Tale is play divided into two halves—tragedy and redemption. It begins in the court of Leontes, King of Sicilia, whose sudden and irrational jealousy leads him to accuse his wife Hermione of betrayal. His actions cause devastating consequences, including the loss of family, friendship, and trust. This play emphasizes the power of jealousy to destroy, but the greater power of love to restore.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8

books.title: Denison Algebra 1
books.author: Denison
books.rationale: Denison offers a complete high school math program with video lessons taught by a veteran teacher and homeschool dad for Pre-Algebra through Algebra 2. Pre-algebra only requires familiarity with the four basic functions (add, subtract, multiply, and divide). After Pre-Algebra, the course splits into two tracks for Algebra, Geometry, and Algebra 2: a track for students who don't have significant struggles but aren't enjoying math, and a Success track for students who struggle with math and need shorter lessons with more scaffolding.
books.noteText:
Choose this or Elementary Algebra.
If following the Denison track, you only need to purchase this text for 8th-grade math. Use this in place of Elementary Algebra AND RightStart Geometry.

books.title: Song in a Rainstorm: The Story of Musical Prodigy Thomas "Blind Tom" Wiggins
books.author: Glenda Armand
books.rationale: "Had he been born in our time," the author writes, "he might have been called an autistic musical savant." Students will enjoy this picture book biography of an incredible American performer and composer during the last half of the 19th century.
books.noteText: For Composer Study Term 3, choose either Blind Tom OR Modest Mussorgsky's "Pictures at an Exhibition" to study for the first part of the term. Blind Tom is recommended for younger students.

books.title: The Story of Science: Newton At the Center
books.author: Joy Hakim
books.rationale: This series situates facts and theories within their historical, political, and cultural context.  The author tells the story of (physical) science as a process of developing knowledge that is intimately tied to human society. Ms. Hakim challenges readers to consider differing viewpoints, so as to understand science with uncommon wholeness.
books.noteText:
Sensitive content. Lesson plans provide support, but teachers might choose to pre-read and plan how to handle potentially controversial topics. 

Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Learning Ally with VoiceText.

books.title: Invincible Microbe
books.author: Jim Murphy
books.rationale: Murphy tells the story of an ancient and elusive microbe, weaving together ideas from science, history, and ethics. This author is a master storyteller, and we can't imagine a better way to continue our journey through natural history and into the very timely topic of microbiology and infectious disease.

books.title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat: Mastering the Elements of Good Cooking
books.author: Samin Nosrat
books.rationale: Whether for afternoon occupations or High School Food Science, young cooks will be inspired by this well-written text with its engaging narrative and beautiful illustrations.  The author does a good job of introducing the basic ingredients of cooking without requiring a full chemistry course; students who have already taken chemistry will find additional connections to enhance their understanding even more.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.

books.rationale: Travel through the history of architecture in _The LEGO Architect_. Learn about styles like Art Deco, Modernism, and High-Tech, and find inspiration in galleries of LEGO models. Then take a turn building 12 models in a variety of styles.
